channel.title: Posts on DevOps & SRE
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on DevOps & SRE

item.title: Kind & Terraform
item.description: Kind kind is a tool for running local Kubernetes clusters using Docker container “nodes”. kind was primarily designed for testing Kubernetes itself, but may be used for local development or CI. We still start a series of articles of how to manage Kubernetes resources via Terraform. First we need a Kubernetes cluster running locally. There are many tools to pick from (minikube, K3s, MicroK8s etc..) , and the tool we will use is Kind.
item.content:
Kind

kind is a tool for running local Kubernetes clusters using Docker container “nodes”. kind was primarily designed for testing Kubernetes itself, but may be used for local development or CI.

We still start a series of articles of how to manage Kubernetes resources via Terraform.

First we need a Kubernetes cluster running locally. There are many tools to pick from (minikube, K3s, MicroK8s etc..) , and the tool we will use is Kind.

To use kind, you will also need to install docker.

Even though we can easily create a new cluster by running kind create cluster we will be doing this by using Terraform

item.title: Hugo
item.description: Hugo in 5 minutes Get a GitHub Account Create a GitHub repository and name it <username>.github.io. Create a Hugo project hugo new site . --force Pick a (Hugo) theme from here Add content to your new blog. hugo new posts/hugo.md Generate the blog by running hugo Push to GitHub Enable GitHub Pages Visit your published blog at https://.github.io
item.content:
Hugo in 5 minutes

  1. Get a GitHub Account
  2. Create a GitHub repository and name it <username>.github.io.
  3. Create a Hugo project hugo new site . --force
  4. Pick a (Hugo) theme from here
  5. Add content to your new blog. hugo new posts/hugo.md
  6. Generate the blog by running hugo
  7. Push to GitHub
  8. Enable GitHub Pages
  9. Visit your published blog at https://.github.io
